name: Tiwanaku Full
description: The 'Tiwanaku Full' concept encompasses various alternative theories surrounding the Tiwanaku civilization (c. 300-1000 AD) in present-day Bolivia. While mainstream archaeology attributes the civilization's achievements to indigenous ingenuity and advanced agricultural techniques for the high-altitude environment, alternative theories often posit that Tiwanaku's accomplishments were due to extraterrestrial influence, a connection to Atlantis, or lost advanced technologies. Proponents point to the precise stonework, the alignment of structures with celestial events, and the seemingly impossible feats of engineering given the available technology of the time. They suggest that the Puma Punku complex, with its massive and precisely cut stones, is evidence of advanced machining beyond the capabilities of the Tiwanaku people as understood by mainstream archaeology. Some also interpret the iconography of the Gate of the Sun as representing advanced knowledge or contact with other civilizations. Mainstream archaeology refutes these claims, arguing that the stonework, while impressive, can be explained by sophisticated but terrestrial engineering techniques involving levers, ramps, and skilled labor. The celestial alignments are also interpreted as evidence of advanced astronomical knowledge within the Tiwanaku culture, rather than extraterrestrial intervention. Furthermore, the collapse of Tiwanaku is attributed to environmental factors like drought and internal social strife, rather than a sudden loss of advanced technology.
subtitle: Alternative theories surrounding the Tiwanaku civilization

claims.claim: Tiwanaku's advanced stonework proves lost technology.
claims.evidence: Puma Punku's precision cuts and massive stones are too advanced for the time.
claims.criticism: Archaeologists demonstrate how ramps, levers, and skilled labor could achieve this stonework.

claims.evidence: Unexplained aspects of the site suggest advanced knowledge beyond Tiwanaku's capabilities.
claims.criticism: Mainstream archaeology explains these aspects through advanced astronomical knowledge and engineering within the culture.

claims.claim: Tiwanaku is connected to Atlantis or other lost civilizations.
claims.evidence: Similarities in architecture and symbolism suggest a shared, ancient source.
claims.criticism: These similarities are often superficial and based on selective interpretation, lacking concrete evidence.

keyFigures.name: Arthur Posnansky
keyFigures.role: Early researcher who proposed an older age for Tiwanaku, influencing later alternative theories.

keyFigures.name: Alan Kolata
keyFigures.role: Leading archaeologist providing mainstream perspectives on Tiwanaku's development and collapse.

mythologyConnections: Connections to Andean cosmology and creation myths

timeline.event: Early researchers begin to propose alternative interpretations of Tiwanaku's origins.
Alternative theories continue to circulate, often online and in popular culture.

sources.author: Posnansky, Arthur
sources.work: Tihuanacu, the Cradle of American Man
sources.title: Posnansky, Arthur

sources.author: Kolata, Alan L.
sources.work: The Tiwanaku: Portrait of a Andean Civilization
sources.title: Kolata, Alan L.

sources.author: Childress, David Hatcher
sources.work: Technology of the Gods: The Incredible Sciences of the Ancients
sources.title: Childress, David Hatcher
